authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Carson City, general "All waters" listing
sources.quotedText: CARSON CITY. CLOSED WATERS: None. OPEN WATERS: All waters (Except as noted below). Open year around. Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye. Any hour of the day or night

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Carson City special-water listings (only 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' and 'Baily Fishing Pond' are broken out; Ash Canyon Creek is not named and is not a Lake Tahoe tributary)
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around. Daily: 5 game fish. Possession: 10 game fish. 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset. Baily Fishing Pond. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours park is open to public use.

sources.title: Ash Canyon Creek — Nevada Department of Wildlife (Carson City, Western Region, "Creek or Stream")
sources.quotedText: County: Carson City. Region: Western. Type of Water: Creek or Stream.

sources.title: OpenStreetMap way 1382371793 — "Ash Canyon Creek" (waterway=stream), a mapped stream on the east slope of the Carson Range draining east/downhill into the Carson City / Eagle Valley floor (not a Lake Tahoe tributary), Carson City, NV
Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 858751, "Ash Canyon Creek", Stream, Carson City (city), NV
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":858751,"gaz_name":"Ash Canyon Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Carson City (city)"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13333622.8815, 4745800.3981) converts to lon -119.77797, lat 39.16825, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-119.778, 39.1682) to within ~5m.

parameters.note: Ash Canyon Creek is a Carson Range stream on the west side of Carson City, Nevada (NDOW catalogs it under Carson City). It runs east/downhill out of the range into the Carson City / Eagle Valley floor (the Carson River drainage) — it does NOT flow into Lake Tahoe, so it is not covered by the Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' special row, and it is not the separately-listed Baily Fishing Pond. Carson City has no closed waters, and Ash Canyon Creek is not individually listed, so the governing rule is the jurisdiction's general 'All waters (Except as noted below)' listing — open year around.

regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Carson City general 'All waters' listing; Ash Canyon Creek is an east-slope Carson City stream, not a Lake Tahoe tributary, and is not individually listed).
regulations.verbatimText: All waters (Except as noted below). Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: Carson City general limit: Daily 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye); Possession 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, and 30 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye). On a small trout creek the practical limit is 5 trout per day / 10 in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily / 10 in possession (plus the jurisdiction's separate mountain-whitefish and warmwater allowances) under the Carson City general 'All waters' limit.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye.

parameters.note: Under the Carson City general 'All waters' listing, fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night (the restricted 1-hour-before-sunrise-to-2-hours-after-sunset window applies only to the Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' row, which does not cover Ash Canyon Creek).

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. Ash Canyon Creek is a Nevada (Carson City) inland stream, so a standard Nevada resident/nonresident license applies.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
